seoTitle: Unisplendour Technical Support | FPGA, Security, Power Management | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Access Unisplendour technical documentation, application notes, and design guides. Authorized distributor support for FPGA development, security chip design, and power management.

faqs.question: What technical support does BeiLuo provide for Unisplendour products?
faqs.answer: As an authorized Unisplendour distributor, BeiLuo provides comprehensive technical support including application engineering assistance, design reviews, debugging support, reference designs, and on-site engineering services. Our FAE team has deep expertise in all Unisplendour product families.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact BeiLuo FAE team for any technical questions or design assistance needs.

faqs.question: How do I access Unisplendour development tools?
faqs.answer: Development tools including Pango Design Suite for FPGA development can be downloaded from Unisplendour's website. BeiLuo can provide evaluation kits, programming tools, and licensing assistance for development environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact BeiLuo for evaluation kit recommendations and tool licensing support.

faqs.question: Are reference designs available for Unisplendour products?
faqs.answer: Yes, Unisplendour provides comprehensive reference designs for all major product families. These include schematics, PCB layouts, BOMs, and firmware. BeiLuo provides these reference designs as part of our distributor support services.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request reference designs from BeiLuo early in your design cycle to accelerate development.

faqs.question: What documentation is available for getting started?
faqs.answer: Unisplendour provides datasheets, user manuals, application notes, and quick start guides for all products. BeiLuo maintains a comprehensive library of technical documentation and can provide additional application-specific guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with datasheet and quick start guide. Contact BeiLuo for application-specific documentation.

faqs.question: How can I get training on Unisplendour products?
faqs.answer: BeiLuo offers product training sessions for customers, covering FPGA development, security chip design, power management, and industrial control applications. Training can be conducted on-site or remotely depending on your needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact BeiLuo to schedule training sessions for your engineering team.

articles.title: Unisplendour FPGA Development Quick Start Guide
articles.summary: Complete guide to getting started with Unisplendour Titan and Logos FPGA families, including tool installation, project creation, and first design implementation.

content.text: This guide provides step-by-step instructions for setting up your development environment and creating your first FPGA design using Unisplendour devices. Whether you are new to FPGA development or migrating from another vendor, this guide will help you get started quickly.
Development Tools Installation

Unisplendour FPGAs are supported by the Pango Design Suite, a comprehensive development environment that includes synthesis, placement and routing, simulation, and debugging tools. Download the latest version from the Unisplendour website and follow the installation wizard.

items: Download Pango Design Suite from the support portal
Run the installer with administrator privileges
Select device families you plan to use (Titan, Logos, Compact)
Install USB drivers for programming cable

content.text: Creating Your First Project

Launch Pango Design Suite and create a new project. Select your target device from the device selector, which organizes parts by family and package type. Define your project settings including top-level module name and default language (Verilog or VHDL).
Design Entry and Synthesis

Enter your design using the integrated text editor or schematic capture tool. The synthesis tool optimizes your RTL code for Unisplendour FPGA architecture, mapping logic to LUTs, registers, and dedicated resources like DSP slices and block RAMs.
Implementation and Programming

After successful synthesis, run implementation which includes placement, routing, and bitstream generation. Use the integrated programmer to download the bitstream to your target board via JTAG or configuration memory interface.

author.name: Unisplendour Technical Team
author.title: Senior Applications Engineer
author.bio: Expert in FPGA design and development with 10+ years of experience in programmable logic solutions.

relatedArticles.title: Secure Chip Application Design Best Practices

faeInsights.summary: This guide covers the essential steps for getting started with Unisplendour FPGA development. The key is to start with a reference design and modify it for your specific requirements.
faeInsights.decisionLogic: 1. Install Tools: Get Pango Design Suite first. 2. Start Simple: Use reference designs as starting point. 3. Iterate: Build and test incrementally. 4. Leverage Support: Contact BeiLuo FAE when stuck.
faeInsights.keyConsiderations: Tool version compatibility, device selection for your logic requirements, and proper constraint files are critical for successful FPGA development.
commonPitfalls: Insufficient timing constraints leading to setup/hold violations
Incorrect pin assignments causing signal integrity issues
Inadequate simulation coverage missing corner cases
Poor power supply decoupling affecting signal integrity

customerCases.challenge: Migrating from Xilinx to Unisplendour FPGA
customerCases.solution: Followed this quick start guide with BeiLuo FAE support
customerCases.feedback: The guide and BeiLuo support made the migration smooth. We were productive within two weeks.

faq.question: What operating systems are supported by Pango Design Suite?
faq.answer: Pango Design Suite supports Windows 10/11 (64-bit) and select Linux distributions including Ubuntu 20.04 LTS and CentOS 7.
faq.decisionGuide: Use Windows for easiest setup. Linux available for enterprise environments.

faq.question: Can I import designs from other FPGA vendors?
faq.answer: Yes, RTL code (Verilog/VHDL) is portable. IP cores may need adaptation using Unisplendour's IP catalog equivalents.
faq.decisionGuide: RTL is portable. Plan for IP core migration. Contact BeiLuo for IP alternatives.

faq.question: What debugging tools are available?
faq.answer: Pango Design Suite includes integrated logic analyzer (ChipWatcher) for real-time signal monitoring and debugging.
faq.decisionGuide: Use ChipWatcher for debugging. Add debug probes early in design process.

articles.title: Secure Chip Application Design Best Practices
articles.summary: Best practices for designing secure applications with Unisplendour THD series security chips, covering secure boot, key management, and tamper resistance.

content.text: Security chips require careful design considerations beyond standard microcontroller applications. This application note covers essential security concepts and practical implementation guidelines for Unisplendour THD89 and THD20 secure microcontrollers.
Security Architecture Overview

Unisplendour security chips feature multiple security layers including hardware cryptographic accelerators, secure memory regions, tamper detection circuits, and secure boot mechanisms. Understanding these features is essential for designing robust security applications.
Secure Boot Implementation

Implement secure boot to ensure only authenticated firmware runs on your device. The secure boot process verifies firmware signatures using embedded public keys before execution, preventing unauthorized code execution.

items: Generate RSA/ECC key pairs for code signing
Configure secure boot fuses during production

content.text: Key Management Guidelines

Proper key management is critical for security. Use the chip's secure key storage for sensitive keys, implement key diversification for multi-device deployments, and establish secure key injection procedures during manufacturing.
Tamper Resistance Design

Utilize the chip's tamper detection features including voltage monitoring, temperature sensing, and physical attack detection. Configure appropriate response actions such as key erasure or device lockdown when tampering is detected.

author.name: Unisplendour Security Team
author.title: Principal Security Architect
author.bio: Expert in hardware security with 15+ years of experience in secure chip design and Common Criteria certification.

relatedArticles.title: Unisplendour FPGA Development Quick Start Guide

faeInsights.summary: Security design requires a systematic approach. This guide provides best practices for implementing secure applications with THD series chips.
faeInsights.decisionLogic: 1. Threat Analysis: Identify attack vectors first. 2. Defense in Depth: Multiple security layers. 3. Key Management: Secure generation, storage, and injection. 4. Tamper Response: Appropriate actions for detected attacks.
faeInsights.keyConsiderations: Security certification requirements, threat model, key lifecycle management, and tamper response policies are critical design factors.
commonPitfalls: Weak key management practices compromising security
Inadequate secure boot implementation allowing unauthorized code
Poor random number generation affecting cryptographic strength

customerCases.challenge: Designing PCI PTS compliant payment terminal
customerCases.solution: Implemented THD89 with secure boot and tamper protection following this guide
customerCases.feedback: The security best practices helped us achieve PCI PTS certification on first submission.

faq.question: What cryptographic algorithms are supported?
faq.answer: THD89 supports AES, DES/3DES, RSA, ECC, SHA-1/256, and SM2/SM3/SM4 algorithms in hardware.
faq.decisionGuide: All major algorithms supported. Use AES-256 for symmetric, RSA-2048 or ECC P-256 for asymmetric.

faq.question: How do I handle key injection securely?
faq.answer: Use certified secure personalization facilities with HSM-protected key injection processes. Unisplendour offers personalization services.
faq.decisionGuide: Use certified facilities. BeiLuo offers personalization services for production.

faq.question: What certifications do these chips have?
faq.answer: THD89 is CC EAL6+ certified. THD20 is CC EAL5+ certified. Both meet PCI PTS and EMVCo requirements.
faq.decisionGuide: THD89 for highest security (banking). THD20 for cost-sensitive secure applications.

articles.title: Power Management IC Design Guidelines
articles.summary: Comprehensive design guidelines for implementing Unisplendour power management ICs including buck converters, LDOs, and battery management solutions.

content.text: Proper power supply design is critical for system reliability and performance. This guide covers design considerations, component selection, and layout guidelines for Unisplendour power management ICs.
Buck Converter Design

The USD3410 synchronous buck converter offers high efficiency and compact solution size. Key design parameters include inductor selection, input/output capacitor sizing, and compensation network design for stable operation.

items: Select inductor based on ripple current requirements (typically 30-40% of Iout)
Use ceramic capacitors with adequate voltage rating and temperature characteristics
Place input capacitor close to VIN and GND pins to minimize switching noise
Design compensation network based on output capacitor ESR and load conditions
Follow recommended PCB layout guidelines for thermal performance

content.text: LDO Application Design

The USL1801 low-dropout regulator is ideal for noise-sensitive applications. Design considerations include dropout voltage, ground current, power dissipation, and stability with various output capacitors.
Thermal Management

Calculate power dissipation and junction temperature to ensure reliable operation. Use thermal vias and adequate copper area for heat dissipation. Consider ambient temperature and airflow in your design.
PCB Layout Recommendations
Good PCB layout is essential for power supply performance. Minimize loop areas for switching currents, use solid ground planes, and keep sensitive analog circuits away from switching nodes.

author.name: Unisplendour Applications Team
author.title: Senior Power Applications Engineer
author.bio: Specialist in power management IC design with expertise in DC-DC converters and LDO regulators.

relatedArticles.title: Unisplendour FPGA Development Quick Start Guide
Secure Chip Application Design Best Practices

faeInsights.summary: Power supply design requires attention to component selection, thermal management, and PCB layout. This guide provides practical guidelines for successful designs.
faeInsights.decisionLogic: 1. Topology: Choose buck for high efficiency, LDO for low noise. 2. Components: Select based on current, voltage, and ripple requirements. 3. Thermal: Calculate dissipation and ensure adequate heatsinking. 4. Layout: Minimize loop areas and use proper grounding.
faeInsights.keyConsiderations: Input/output voltage range, load current, efficiency requirements, noise specifications, and thermal constraints are critical design factors.
commonPitfalls: Inadequate input capacitance causing voltage ripple
Poor inductor selection leading to saturation
Noisy PCB layout affecting output regulation

customerCases.challenge: Designing efficient power supply for battery-operated device
customerCases.solution: Used USD3410 buck converter with USL1801 LDO for noise-sensitive RF section
customerCases.feedback: The design guidelines helped us achieve 95% efficiency and extended battery life by 40%.

faq.question: What is the recommended inductor for USD3410?
faq.answer: For 3A output, use 2.2μH to 4.7μH inductor with saturation current >4A and DCR <50mΩ. Shielded inductors recommended for EMI-sensitive applications.
faq.decisionGuide: Start with 4.7μH for general applications. Lower value for smaller size, higher for lower ripple.

faq.question: Can I parallel multiple USD3410 for higher current?
faq.answer: Yes, USD3410 supports current sharing mode. Connect SYNC pins together and follow the parallel operation guidelines in the datasheet.
faq.decisionGuide: Use parallel configuration for >3A loads. Ensure proper current sharing and thermal management.

faq.question: What capacitor types are recommended for LDO output?
faq.answer: USL1801 is stable with ceramic capacitors (X5R/X7R) from 1μF to 22μF. Avoid Y5V capacitors due to poor temperature characteristics.
faq.decisionGuide: Use X5R or X7R ceramic capacitors. 10μF is typical for most applications.

articles.title: Stepper Motor Driver Application Guide
articles.summary: Practical guide for implementing stepper motor control using Unisplendour USMD2208 dual stepper motor driver with microstepping and current control features.

content.text: The USMD2208 dual stepper motor driver provides an integrated solution for controlling two stepper motors with advanced features including microstepping, decay mode control, and overcurrent protection. This guide covers application circuits, configuration, and troubleshooting.
Device Overview

USMD2208 integrates two complete stepper motor drivers with PWM current control, supporting full-step to 1/16-step microstepping modes. Each channel can deliver up to 2.0A peak current with integrated sense resistors.
Current Setting and Microstepping

Motor current is set through VREF voltage and internal sense resistor. Microstepping mode is configured via MODE pins to achieve smooth motion and reduce vibration. Higher microstepping provides smoother operation but requires higher step frequencies.

items: Calculate VREF based on desired motor current: VREF = I_chop × 0.5
Select microstepping mode based on application requirements
Configure decay mode (slow, fast, or mixed) for optimal performance
Set chopper frequency based on motor inductance and supply voltage

content.text: Application Circuit Design

The basic application circuit requires minimal external components. Connect motor power supply (8-35V) to VM, logic supply (3.3V or 5V) to VIO, and motor windings to output pins. Add bulk capacitance near the VM pin for stable operation.
Thermal Considerations

Power dissipation depends on motor current, supply voltage, and duty cycle. Use thermal vias to spread heat to PCB inner layers. Consider adding a heatsink or reducing motor current if the junction temperature exceeds safe limits.

author.name: Unisplendour Motor Control Team
author.title: Motor Control Applications Engineer
author.bio: Expert in motor control systems with extensive experience in stepper and servo motor driver design.

relatedArticles.title: Unisplendour FPGA Development Quick Start Guide
Secure Chip Application Design Best Practices

faeInsights.summary: Stepper motor driver design requires proper current setting, microstepping configuration, and thermal management. This guide provides practical implementation guidelines.
faeInsights.decisionLogic: 1. Current Setting: Match to motor rating for optimal torque. 2. Microstepping: Higher for smoother motion, lower for more torque. 3. Decay Mode: Fast for high speed, slow for low speed. 4. Thermal: Ensure adequate heatsinking for continuous operation.
faeInsights.keyConsiderations: Motor current rating, supply voltage, microstepping requirements, and thermal constraints are critical design factors.
commonPitfalls: Incorrect current setting causing motor overheating
Inadequate thermal design leading to thermal shutdown
Poor PCB layout causing noise and EMI issues
Improper decay mode selection causing motor vibration

customerCases.challenge: Reducing motor noise and vibration in 3D printers
customerCases.solution: Implemented USMD2208 with 1/16 microstepping and optimized decay mode
customerCases.feedback: Motor noise reduced by 80% and print quality improved significantly.

faq.question: What motor voltage can USMD2208 support?
faq.answer: USMD2208 supports motor supply voltage from 8V to 35V. Ensure proper voltage rating for your specific motor.
faq.decisionGuide: Match supply voltage to motor rating. Higher voltage allows higher speed but increases heating.

faq.question: How do I reduce motor noise and vibration?
faq.answer: Use higher microstepping modes (1/8 or 1/16), adjust decay mode settings, and implement smooth acceleration/deceleration profiles.
faq.decisionGuide: Start with 1/16 microstepping. Adjust decay mode based on speed requirements.

faq.question: What protection features are built-in?
faq.answer: USMD2208 includes overcurrent protection, thermal shutdown, undervoltage lockout, and short-circuit protection for motor outputs.
faq.decisionGuide: All protections are automatic. Ensure proper PCB layout for reliable protection operation.
